english: From Latin 'identitas' (sameness). 'Wer bin ich?' is the\nfundamental Identitätsfrage in German discourse.

From Latin 'identitas' (sameness). The Fugen-s creates compounds: Identitätskrise (identity crisis), Identitätspolitik (identity politics). In German, Identität covers personal, cultural, and national dimensions.
'Bewusst' (aware) + 'sein' (being). Literally: the state\nof being aware. Philosophers love this word.

'Bewusst' (aware) + 'sein' (being). Literally: the state of being aware. Philosophers from Hegel to Habermas have written volumes about Bewusstsein. 'Kulturelles Bewusstsein' (cultural awareness) is a modern educational goal.
'Her' (from/hither) + 'Kunft' (coming). Where you come\nfrom. 'Herkunftsland' appears on every German form.
'Her' (from/hither) + 'Kunft' (coming). 'Herkunftsland' (country of origin) appears on every German form. Your Herkunft shapes but need not define you.
From Latin 'traditio' (handing over). Same word internationally.\nGermans distinguish Brauch (living custom) from Tradition.
From Latin 'traditio' (handing over). Germans distinguish between living traditions (Bräuche) and inherited customs (Überlieferungen). 'Traditionen pflegen' means to maintain traditions actively.
'Viel' (many) + 'Falt' (fold). Literally: many-foldedness.\n'Kulturelle Vielfalt' is celebrated in modern German politics.
'Viel' (many) + 'Falt' (fold). Literally: many-foldedness. 'Kulturelle Vielfalt' (cultural diversity) is celebrated in modern German political discourse. The word implies richness in difference.
From Latin 'tolerantia' (endurance). Lessing's play 'Nathan\nder Weise' (1779) made Toleranz a central German value.
From Latin 'tolerantia' (endurance). Lessing's play 'Nathan der Weise' (1779) made Toleranz a central German value. The Ring Parable argues all religions deserve equal respect.
'Vor' (before) + 'Urteil' (judgment). A judgment made\nbefore knowing the facts. The word reveals the problem.
'Vor' (before) + 'Urteil' (judgment). A judgment made before knowing the facts. 'Vorurteile abbauen' (to reduce prejudices). The word itself reveals the problem: judging before examining.
'Wahr' (true) + 'Nehmung' (taking). Literally: taking in\ntruth. How you perceive the world around you.
'Wahr' (true) + 'Nehmung' (taking). Literally: taking in truth. 'Die Wahrnehmung verändert sich' (perception changes). A philosophical powerhouse of a word.
'Zu' (to) + 'gehören' (to belong) + '-igkeit.' The feeling\nof being part of something. What every immigrant seeks.
'Zu' (to) + 'gehören' (to belong) + '-igkeit.' The feeling of being part of something. 'Ein Gefühl der Zugehörigkeit' (a sense of belonging). What every immigrant seeks.
'Zusammen' (together) + 'Leben' (life/living). 'Friedliches\nZusammenleben' is peaceful coexistence. An ongoing project.
'Zusammen' (together) + 'Leben' (life/living). 'Friedliches Zusammenleben' (peaceful coexistence). Germany's multicultural Zusammenleben is an ongoing project that shapes daily life.
'Gesellschaft' (society) + '-lich.' More formal than 'sozial.'\nGesellschaftlich addresses the structure of society.

'Gesellschaft' (society) + '-lich.' More formal than 'sozial.' Gesellschaftlich addresses the structure; sozial addresses the human aspect. 'Gesellschaftliche Veränderungen' (societal changes) shape the big picture.
'Multi' (many) + 'kulturell' (cultural). Germany debated\nfor decades whether it was multikulturell. The answer: yes.
'Multi' (many) + 'kulturell' (cultural). Germany debated for decades whether it was multikulturell. The answer: yes, whether anyone planned it or not. Berlin is the prime example.
'Inter' (between) + 'kulturell.' Between cultures. Multi-\nmeans many cultures existing. Inter- means cultures interacting.

'Inter' (between) + 'kulturell.' 'Interkulturelle Kompetenz' (intercultural competence) is a formal job qualification in Germany. Multi- means many cultures existing. Inter- means cultures interacting.
From Latin 'tolerans.' Same word internationally.\n'Tolerant sein' is easier said than practiced.
From Latin 'tolerans.' Same word internationally. The adjective form of Toleranz. 'Eine tolerante Gesellschaft' (a tolerant society). 'Tolerant sein' is easier said than practiced.
'Tradition' + '-ell.' The -ell ending comes from French\nand marks formal adjectives. Can be positive or conservative.
'Tradition' + '-ell.' The -ell ending comes from French and marks formal adjectives. Traditionell can be positive (heritage) or conservative (resistance to change).
From Latin 'reflectere' (to bend back). Both literal (light)\nand figurative (thought). Non-separable -ieren verb.
From Latin 'reflectere' (to bend back). Both literal (light) and figurative (thought). Non-separable -ieren verb. 'Über seine Identität reflektieren' (to reflect on one's identity).
'Wahr' (true) + 'nehmen' (to take). Separable: 'Ich nehme\nwahr.' Taking in what is true. Active observation.
'Wahr' (true) + 'nehmen' (to take). Separable: 'Ich nehme wahr.' Taking in what is true. Active observation. The noun Wahrnehmung is one of Germany's great philosophical words.
'Hinter' (behind) + 'fragen' (to ask). Not separable despite\nhinter-. You ask behind the surface. Going deeper.
'Hinter' (behind) + 'fragen' (to ask). Not separable despite hinter-. You ask behind the surface. Going deeper than a simple Frage. 'Traditionen hinterfragen' (to question traditions critically).
'An' (at) + 'Schauung' (viewing). 'Weltanschauung' (worldview)\nis one of Germany's most famous word exports.
'An' (at) + 'Schauung' (viewing). 'Weltanschauung' (worldview) is one of Germany's most famous exports. Kant used Anschauung as a philosophical term for intuitive perception.
'Über' (over) + 'Zeugung' (producing). Literally: producing\nsomething overwhelming. Stronger than Meinung (opinion).
'Über' (over) + 'Zeugung' (producing). Literally: producing something overwhelming. 'Aus Überzeugung handeln' (to act from conviction). Stronger than Meinung (opinion).
'Vor' (before) + 'Stellung' (position). At B2 level, the\nabstract meaning dominates: your idea of something.
'Vor' (before) + 'Stellung' (position). Already known as 'introduction/interview' from B1. At B2: the abstract meaning dominates. 'Meine Vorstellung von Glück' (my idea of happiness).
'Wert' (value) + 'Vorstellung' (concept). No Fugenelement\nbetween the parts. Your ideas about what matters most.
'Wert' (value) + 'Vorstellung' (concept). The Fugen-absence here is noteworthy: Wert + Vorstellung without a connecting element. Your ideas about what matters.
'Selbst' (self) + 'Bild' (image). Contrast: Fremdbild (how\nothers see you). The gap between the two is where crises live.
'Selbst' (self) + 'Bild' (image). 'Sein Selbstbild verändern' (to change one's self-image). Contrast: Fremdbild (how others see you). The gap between the two is where identity crises live.
From Latin 'conceptum' (something conceived). In German,\nKonzept also means 'rough draft.' Both senses: something formed.
From Latin 'conceptum' (something conceived). In German, Konzept also means 'rough draft.' 'Ein Konzept entwickeln' (to develop a concept). Both senses share the idea of something being formed.
From Greek 'philosophia' (love of wisdom). Germany produced\nKant, Hegel, Marx, Nietzsche, Heidegger, Arendt.
From Greek 'philosophia' (love of wisdom). Germany produced Kant, Hegel, Marx, Nietzsche, Heidegger, Arendt. 'Deutsche Philosophie' is a major academic tradition.
From Greek 'ethike' (moral philosophy). 'Ethik-Kommission'\nreviews medical research and AI projects in Germany.
From Greek 'ethike' (moral philosophy). 'Ethik-Kommission' (ethics committee) reviews medical research and AI projects in Germany. 'Berufsethik' (professional ethics) is taken seriously.
'Grund' (foundation) + 'Wert' (value). The Grundwerte of\nthe Grundgesetz: human dignity, freedom, equality.
'Grund' (foundation) + 'Wert' (value). 'Die Grundwerte des Grundgesetzes' (the fundamental values of the constitution): human dignity, freedom, equality. Foundation values that cannot be changed.
'Leben' (life) + 's' (Fugen-s) + 'Qualität' (quality).\nGerman cities regularly rank among the highest worldwide.
'Leben' (life) + 's' (Fugen-s) + 'Qualität' (quality). The Fugen-s connects the two parts. German cities regularly rank among the highest for Lebensqualität worldwide.
'Menschen' (human) + 'Würde' (dignity). Article 1 of the\nGerman constitution: 'Die Würde des Menschen ist unantastbar.'
'Menschen' (human) + 'Würde' (dignity). Article 1 of the German constitution: 'Die Würde des Menschen ist unantastbar' (Human dignity is inviolable). The most important sentence in German law.
'Eigen' (own) + 'ständig' (standing). You stand on your own.\nNot relying on others for your opinions or identity.
'Eigen' (own) + 'ständig' (standing). 'Eigenständig denken' (to think independently). You stand on your own. Not relying on others for your opinions or identity.
Same Germanic root as English 'open.' Karl Popper's\nconcept of the 'open society' deeply influenced Germany.
Same Germanic root as English 'open.' 'Offen für Neues sein' (to be open to new things). Karl Popper's concept of the 'open society' deeply influenced German postwar thought.
'Respekt' (respect) + 'voll' (full). Full of respect. The\n-voll suffix works like English '-ful': liebevoll, hoffnungsvoll.
'Respekt' (respect) + 'voll' (full). Full of respect. The -voll suffix works like English '-ful': liebevoll (loving), hoffnungsvoll (hopeful), respektvoll (respectful).
'Welt' (world) + 'offen' (open). Berlin calls itself weltoffen.\nThe word captures Germany's self-image as globally minded.
'Welt' (world) + 'offen' (open). 'Eine weltoffene Stadt' (a cosmopolitan city). Berlin calls itself weltoffen. The word captures Germany's self-image as globally minded.
'Viel' (many) + 'fältig' (fold). Many-folded, multi-layered,\nrich in variety. The adjective form of Vielfalt.
'Viel' (many) + 'fältig' (fold). Related to Vielfalt. The adjective form. Many-folded, multi-layered, rich in variety. 'Eine vielfältige Kultur' (a diverse culture).
From Latin 'respectare' (to look back at). Non-separable\n-ieren verb. Same word across European languages.
From Latin 'respectare' (to look back at). 'Andere Kulturen respektieren' (to respect other cultures). Non-separable -ieren verb. Same word across European languages.
'Die Kindheit prägt den Menschen' (Childhood shapes a person).\nAlso: 'eine Münze prägen' (to mint a coin). Deeply formative.
Something stamped into you that remains. 'Die Kindheit prägt den Menschen' (Childhood shapes a person). Also: 'eine Münze prägen' (to mint a coin). Deeply formative.
'Traditionen pflegen' (to cultivate traditions). Also:\n'Kranke pflegen' (to care for the sick). Nurturing in all forms.
'Traditionen pflegen' (to cultivate traditions). Also: 'Kranke pflegen' (to care for the sick). 'Pflegeversicherung' (nursing care insurance) is one of Germany's five social insurances.
'An' (at) + 'erkennen' (to recognize). Not separable despite\nthe an-. The official stamp of acceptance.
'An' (at) + 'erkennen' (to recognize). 'Einen Abschluss anerkennen' (to recognize a degree). Not separable despite the an-. The official stamp of acceptance.
From Latin 'migratio' (wandering). Germany's 2015\nMigrationsdebatte changed the political landscape.
From Latin 'migratio' (wandering). Germany's 2015 Migrationsdebatte changed the political landscape. 'Migrationshintergrund' (migration background) is a German census category: 21 million people.
From Latin 'integratio' (renewal). 'Integration ist keine\nEinbahnstraße' (not a one-way street) is a common German saying.
From Latin 'integratio' (renewal/restoration). 'Integration ist keine Einbahnstraße' (Integration is not a one-way street) is a common German saying. Both sides must move.
'Ein' (in) + 'Wanderung' (wandering). Germany was officially\n'kein Einwanderungsland' until 2005. Reality preceded policy.

'Ein' (in) + 'Wanderung' (wandering). Germany was officially 'kein Einwanderungsland' (not a country of immigration) until 2005 when it passed its first immigration law. Reality preceded policy by decades.
From Latin 'discriminare' (to distinguish). The AGG (General\nEqual Treatment Act) of 2006 prohibits it in employment.
From Latin 'discriminare' (to distinguish). The Allgemeines Gleichbehandlungsgesetz (AGG, General Equal Treatment Act) of 2006 prohibits Diskriminierung in employment and daily life.
From Latin 'inclusio' (enclosure). Beyond disability:\n'Inklusion means everyone belongs.' Active inclusion.
From Latin 'inclusio' (enclosure). Beyond disability: Inklusion means everyone belongs. Germany's education system is moving toward Inklusion in schools. Not just tolerance, but active inclusion.
From French 'milieu' (middle/environment). Sinus-Milieus\ndivide German society into lifestyle groups.
From French 'milieu' (middle/environment). Sinus-Milieus divide German society into lifestyle groups: liberal-intellectual, traditional, performer, etc. Your Milieu shapes your Weltanschauung.
'Generationen' (generations) + 'Konflikt.' The Fugen-en\nconnects the parts. Every generation has its defining conflict.
'Generationen' (generations) + 'Konflikt.' Every generation in Germany has its defining conflict: 68ers vs. parents, Gen Z vs. Boomers on climate. The Fugen-en connects the parts.
From 'wandeln' (to change/walk). Gradual, deep change: not\na revolution but an evolution. Wandel takes time.
From 'wandeln' (to change/walk). Wandel implies gradual, deep change: not a revolution but an evolution. 'Wandel durch Annäherung' (change through rapprochement) was Willy Brandt's Ostpolitik.
'Lebens' (life's) + 'Weise' (way/manner). The Fugen-s links\nLeben to Weise. How you choose to live your life.
'Lebens' (life's) + 'Weise' (way/manner). The Fugen-s links Leben to Weise. 'Alternative Lebensweisen' (alternative lifestyles) became a political topic in the 1970s.
'Welt' (world) + 'Anschauung' (view). One of the most\nfamous German words exported to English. Kant used it first.
'Welt' (world) + 'Anschauung' (view). One of the most famous German words exported to English. Kant used it first. Your comprehensive framework for understanding everything.
From Greek 'ethnikos' (of a people). Used carefully in German\ndiscourse due to historical sensitivity.
From Greek 'ethnikos' (of a people). 'Ethnische Vielfalt' (ethnic diversity). A term used carefully in German discourse due to historical sensitivity.
'Plural' + '-istisch.' Many worldviews coexisting. The ideal\nof the Grundgesetz: no single ideology dominates.
'Plural' + '-istisch.' Pluralistic. Many worldviews coexisting. The ideal of the Grundgesetz: no single ideology dominates. 'Eine pluralistische Gesellschaft' (a pluralistic society).
From Greek 'kosmopolites' (citizen of the world). Berlin\nconsiders itself kosmopolitisch. Comfort with global diversity.
From Greek 'kosmopolites' (citizen of the world). 'Eine kosmopolitische Einstellung' (a cosmopolitan attitude). Berlin considers itself kosmopolitisch. The word implies comfort with global diversity.
From Latin 'integrare' (to make whole). Non-separable -ieren\nverb. Both transitive and reflexive.
From Latin 'integrare' (to make whole). 'Sich in eine Gesellschaft integrieren' (to integrate into a society). Non-separable -ieren verb. Both transitive and reflexive.
'Auseinander' (apart) + 'setzen' (to set) + 'mit' (with).\nLiterally: to set things apart with something. Intellectual verb.

'Auseinander' (apart) + 'setzen' (to set) + 'mit' (with). Literally: to set things apart with something. 'Sich mit Vorurteilen auseinandersetzen' (to confront prejudices). The most intellectual reflexive verb in German.
'Identifizieren' (to identify) + 'mit' (with). Reflexive.\nFinding yourself in something external.
'Identifizieren' (to identify) + 'mit' (with). Reflexive. 'Sich mit einer Kultur identifizieren' (to identify with a culture). Finding yourself in something external.
'An' (at/to) + 'passen' (to fit). Separable and reflexive.\nFitting yourself to new circumstances without losing yourself.
'An' (at/to) + 'passen' (to fit). Separable: 'Ich passe mich an.' Fitting yourself to new circumstances. Not losing yourself, but adjusting. A survival skill.
'Ver-' + 'Mittel' (middle/means). Being the middle person.\nAlso: 'Wissen vermitteln' (to convey knowledge). A bridge function.
'Ver-' + 'Mittel' (middle/means). 'Zwischen Kulturen vermitteln' (to mediate between cultures). Being the middle person. Also: 'Wissen vermitteln' (to convey knowledge). A bridge function.
Wer/wen/wem folgen den Kasusregeln. Nach alles/nichts/etwas/das immer 'was,' nie 'das.'
Refers to something near or just mentioned. Declines like\na der-word: dieser/diese/dieses/diese. Most common Verweiswort.
Refers to something near or just mentioned. 'Dieser Wert ist wichtig' (This value is important). Declines like a der-word: dieser/diese/dieses/diese. The most common Verweiswort.
Points to something farther away or previously mentioned.\nDeclines like dieser. More literary than spoken.
Points to something farther away or previously mentioned. 'Jener Künstler lebte im 19. Jahrhundert' (That artist lived in the 19th century). Declines like dieser. More literary than spoken.
Describes a kind or type. Declines like dieser. 'Solch ein\nMensch' inverts: solch + ein + noun.
Describes a kind or type. 'Solche Vorurteile sind gefährlich' (Such prejudices are dangerous). Declines like dieser. 'Solch ein Mensch' (such a person) inverts: solch + ein + noun.
'Der' + 'jenige' (the particular one). Compound demonstrative.\nBoth parts decline: desjenigen, demjenigen.
'Der' + 'jenige' (the particular one). 'Derjenige, der zuerst kommt, gewinnt' (The one who comes first wins). Compound demonstrative. Both parts decline: desjenigen, demjenigen.
Feminine form of derjenige. Both parts decline together.\nFormal, written register.
Feminine form of derjenige. 'Diejenige, die am meisten liest, weiß am meisten' (The one (f.) who reads the most knows the most). Both parts decline together.
Neuter form of derjenige. Formal, written. In speech,\nGermans often substitute 'das, was.'
Neuter form of derjenige. 'Dasjenige, was wirklich zählt' (That which truly counts). Formal, written. In speech, Germans often substitute 'das, was.'
'Der' + 'selbe' (same). Both parts decline. Implies true\nidentity: literally the same individual or thing.
'Der' + 'selbe' (same). Both parts decline. Derselbe implies true identity: literally the same individual or thing. 'Derselbe Fehler passiert immer wieder' (The same mistake happens again and again).
Feminine form of derselbe. Not 'die gleiche' (the similar one).\nDerselbe = identical. Der gleiche = similar but separate.
Feminine form of derselbe. 'Dieselbe Frage stellt sich jede Generation' (Every generation faces the same question). Not to be confused with 'die gleiche' (the similar one). Derselbe = identical.
Neuter form of derselbe. 'Ist das dasselbe oder nur das\nGleiche?' is a famous German distinction.

Neuter form of derselbe. 'Ist das dasselbe oder nur das Gleiche?' (Is it the same or just similar?) A famous German distinction. Dasselbe = one and the same object. Das Gleiche = two objects that look alike.
'Erster' (first) + comparative ending. Used in formal writing\nto refer back to the first of two things mentioned.

'Erster' (first) + comparative ending. 'Toleranz und Respekt: Ersterer ist passiv, letzterer aktiv.' Used in formal writing to refer back to two things. Shows you can track two referents across a sentence.
'Letzter' (last) + comparative ending. Classic academic\npairing with ersterer. Avoids repeating nouns. Written register.
'Letzter' (last) + comparative ending. 'Ersterer... letzterer...' is a classic academic pairing. It avoids repeating nouns. Written register. Shows sophisticated text cohesion.
'Ein' + 'solcher.' The indefinite article version of solcher.\nEin solcher Mensch = a person of that kind.
'Ein' + 'solcher.' The indefinite article version of solcher. 'Ein solcher Wandel braucht Zeit' (Such a change takes time). Ein solcher Mensch = a person of that kind.
'Gleicher' (equal) + 'Maßen' (measure). An elegant adverb\nfor balanced comparisons in formal writing.
'Gleicher' (equal) + 'Maßen' (measure). 'Alle profitieren gleichermaßen' (Everyone benefits equally). An elegant adverb for balanced comparisons.
'Im' + 'Wesentlichen' (essential). A qualifier that focuses\non the core. Allows for minor disagreements.
'Im' + 'Wesentlichen' (essential). 'Im Wesentlichen stimme ich zu' (I essentially agree). A qualifier that focuses on the core. Allows for minor disagreements.
'Im' + 'Grunde' (ground/foundation). Looks at the foundation\nbeneath the surface. What lies at the very bottom.
